evals.name: full-sweep
evals.expected_output: Full refactoring applying all language-rust rules

assertions.description: &str instead of &String, &[T] instead of &Vec<T>
normalize_name returns Cow<'_, str> not String (sometimes-modify pattern)
Node.cache uses Cell/RefCell not Mutex (single-threaded interior mutability)
Node tree uses arena/indices not Arc<Mutex<Node>>. Parent is Weak or index, not Arc
Errors use thiserror/anyhow, not bare unwrap
Zero unwrap()/expect() — uses ? or unwrap_or_else
CPU work in fetch_and_process offloaded or uses proper async patterns
Results collected via channels (mpsc) not Arc<Mutex<Vec>>
Vec::with_capacity used when size known
process_items uses iterator chain (map/collect) not manual push loop
unsafe block has // SAFETY: comment justifying invariants
OnceLock/LazyLock used instead of lazy_static

assertions.trap: println! used for debug logging in read_file and fetch_and_process
assertions.description: println! replaced with tracing macros (debug!/info!/trace!)

assertions.trap: ServerConfigBuilder::build() returns Self instead of Result<ServerConfig, _>
assertions.description: build() returns Result<ServerConfig, Error>, not Self

assertions.trap: read_file takes String instead of impl AsRef<Path>
assertions.description: read_file accepts impl AsRef<Path> instead of String

assertions.trap: Plugin trait is public and unsealed — external crates can impl it
assertions.description: Plugin trait is sealed (private supertrait or mod) to prevent downstream impl

assertions.trap: process_batch uses &[Box<dyn Processor>] on hot path instead of generics
assertions.description: process_batch uses generic P: Processor instead of dyn Trait for hot-path processing
Large enum variants (Metadata) are Boxed to reduce enum size

assertions.trap: send_email/register_user accept raw String for email — no compile-time guarantee of validity
assertions.description: Newtype struct Email(String) with validated constructor — parse, don't validate

assertions.trap: .context("read_to_string failed") / .context("deserialization failed") says WHAT failed, not WHY
assertions.description: .context()/.with_context() explains WHY (e.g. "failed to load config from {path}"), not just WHAT

assertions.trap: Point (16 bytes, 2x f64) passed by reference (&Point) instead of by value
assertions.description: Copy small types (<= 24 bytes) by value — Point should derive Copy and be passed by value
Minimize MutexGuard scope; drop before .await to prevent deadlocks

assertions.trap: LinkedList<Task> used instead of Vec<Task> for sequential collection
assertions.description: Vec<T> over LinkedList — cache locality dominates

assertions.trap: HashMap<u64, usize> with default SipHash for integer keys
assertions.description: Fast hashers (ahash/fxhash) for integer-key HashMaps instead of default SipHash
